---
title: "Science"
description: "Research and development and the research workforce"
---

Research, and the knowledge it produces, is an engine of a modern economy and
society — a country's long-run prosperity rests heavily on what it discovers and
learns to do. These charts cover overall research and development spending and the
people who carry that research out. Business-led R&D has its own page:
[Innovation](../innovation/index.qmd).

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
    CATEGORICAL_COLORS, CATEGORICAL_OTHER,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar, lines_over_time, category_bar, stacked_area
```

## Where Canada stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("science").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## R&D expenditure (% of GDP)

Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D (GERD) as a share of GDP — the standard
measure of how much a country invests in research and development across
government, business, and academia.

::: {.callout-note}
**Data lag:** R&D statistics are reported to the OECD with a delay of typically
2–3 years, and countries report at different speeds — Canada often a year or two
ahead of most peers — so the most recent years may not yet have full peer coverage.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
rd_path = "data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv"
if os.path.exists(rd_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(rd_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "rd_pct_gdp",
        title="", yaxis_title="R&D expenditure (% of GDP)", show_average=True,
        initial_visible=["USA"], initial_start=2000)
    fig.update_layout(meta=dict(autoscaleY=True), annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(rd_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("R&D data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest R&D spending: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(rd_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(rd_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "rd_pct_gdp", "R&D expenditure (% of GDP)",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(rd_path)}",
        min_countries=15, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.2f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Researchers per 1,000 employed

The number of researchers (full-time equivalent) per 1,000 people employed. This
measures research *intensity* — the share of the workforce engaged in research —
not research output or quality, which is why countries with smaller workforces and
strong public research (the Nordics, Korea) rank highest.

```{python}
#| echo: false
res_path = "data/science/oecd_researchers.csv"
if os.path.exists(res_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(res_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "researchers_per_1000",
        title="", yaxis_title="Researchers per 1,000 employed", show_average=True,
        initial_start=2000)
    fig.update_layout(meta=dict(autoscaleY=True), annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(res_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Researcher data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest researchers per 1,000: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(res_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(res_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "researchers_per_1000", "Researchers per 1,000 employed",
        f"Source: OECD MSTI | Data as of: {get_data_date(res_path)}",
        min_countries=12, hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Government science funding: the granting agencies

Most federal support for university research flows through three **granting councils** — the Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council (**NSERC**, natural sciences and engineering), the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (**CIHR**, health), and the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council (**SSHRC**). A fourth agency, the **Canada Foundation for Innovation (CFI)** — shown as a separate line you can switch on from the legend — funds research *infrastructure* (labs, equipment and major facilities) rather than operating grants. This tracks their research-and-development funding over time, by federal fiscal year (so **2025 on the axis is 2025/26**; the most recent year is planned spending and may be revised).

The four agencies together are about **$4.3 billion** of the roughly **$10.3 billion** the federal government spends on science and technology R&D. Most of the rest is research performed inside government laboratories — the **National Research Council** is the single largest line — together with the science-based departments (Innovation, Science and Economic Development; the Canadian Space Agency; National Defence; Agriculture; Natural Resources; and others). About seven in ten federal R&D dollars are granted out to universities, businesses and others (*extramural*); the remainder is performed in-house (*intramural*).

Use the menu to switch between **nominal** dollars and **real** (inflation-adjusted, constant 2017) dollars — the gap is the point. Nominal funding has more than doubled since 2000, but adjusted for inflation it is far flatter: after rising through the 2000s, real funding was broadly level across the 2010s, and NSERC's and CIHR's inflation-adjusted funding in the mid-2020s sits close to where it was around 2010. SSHRC has grown more in recent years; the 2020/21 bump reflects one-time pandemic research funding.

```{python}
#| echo: false
sf_path = "data/science/statcan_science_funding.csv"
if os.path.exists(sf_path):
    sf = pd.read_csv(sf_path)
    funders = ["NSERC", "CIHR", "SSHRC", "CFI"]
    sf = sf[sf["agency"].isin(funders)]
    fig = lines_over_time(
        sf, "year", "nominal", "agency",
        yaxis_title="Funding ($ millions)", group_order=funders,
        rangeslider=True, ytickprefix="$", legendonly_groups=["CFI"],
        measures=[
            {"col": "nominal", "label": "Nominal $",
             "yaxis_title": "Funding ($ millions, current)", "ytickprefix": "$", "hover_decimals": 0},
            {"col": "real", "label": "Real (2017 $)",
             "yaxis_title": "Funding ($ millions, constant 2017)", "ytickprefix": "$", "hover_decimals": 0},
        ],
        source_note=(f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 27-10-0026-01 (R&D, total expenditures); "
                     f"real values deflated with Table 27-10-0005-01 | Data as of: {get_data_date(sf_path)}"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Science-funding data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### How the envelope has changed

Federal science and technology R&D has grown from about **$4 billion** in 2000 to over **$10 billion** today. This is the whole envelope over time, grouped into the granting agencies, the National Research Council, and all other departments and in-house science. Switch to **Share of total** to see the composition shift: the granting agencies' slice grew from roughly **28% to over 40%** as more research support was routed through the councils, while the share performed inside departments fell.

```{python}
#| echo: false
env_path = "data/science/statcan_federal_rd_by_agency.csv"
if os.path.exists(env_path):
    sa = pd.read_csv(env_path)
    _grant = {"NSERC", "CIHR", "SSHRC", "CFI"}
    def _band(a):
        if a in _grant:
            return "Granting agencies"
        if a == "National Research Council":
            return "National Research Council"
        return "Other departments & agencies"
    sa["band"] = sa["agency"].map(_band)
    g = sa.groupby(["year", "band"], as_index=False)["nominal"].sum()
    g["share"] = g["nominal"] / g.groupby("year")["nominal"].transform("sum") * 100
    bands = ["Granting agencies", "National Research Council", "Other departments & agencies"]
    fig = stacked_area(
        g, "year", "nominal", "band", yaxis_title="Funding ($ billions)",
        group_order=bands, rangeslider=True,
        measures=[
            {"col": "nominal", "label": "$ billions", "value_scale": 1000,
             "ytickprefix": "$", "hover_decimals": 2, "yaxis_title": "Funding ($ billions)"},
            {"col": "share", "label": "Share of total (%)", "value_scale": 1,
             "yticksuffix": "%", "hover_decimals": 0, "yaxis_title": "Share of total (%)"},
        ],
        source_note=(f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 27-10-0026-01 "
                     f"(R&D, total expenditures) | Data as of: {get_data_date(env_path)}"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Envelope data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### In-house or granted out

The federal government both performs research in its own laboratories (**intramural**) and funds research carried out elsewhere — at universities, in business, and with other partners (**extramural**). In 2000 the split was roughly even. Since then, research performed in-house has grown only slowly while the amount granted out has more than tripled, so today **about seven in ten** federal R&D dollars are spent outside government. Switch to **Share of total** to see the shift from a 50/50 split to roughly 30/70.

```{python}
#| echo: false
perf_path = "data/science/statcan_federal_rd_by_performer.csv"
if os.path.exists(perf_path):
    pf = pd.read_csv(perf_path)
    pf["billions"] = pf["nominal"] / 1000.0
    order = ["Granted out (extramural)", "In-house (intramural)"]
    fig = lines_over_time(
        pf, "year", "billions", "performer", yaxis_title="R&D funding ($ billions)",
        group_order=order, rangeslider=True, ytickprefix="$", hover_decimals=2,
        measures=[
            {"col": "billions", "label": "$ billions", "ytickprefix": "$",
             "hover_decimals": 2, "yaxis_title": "R&D funding ($ billions)"},
            {"col": "share", "label": "Share of total (%)", "ytickprefix": "",
             "yticksuffix": "%", "hover_decimals": 0, "yaxis_title": "Share of federal R&D (%)"},
        ],
        source_note=(f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 27-10-0026-01 "
                     f"(R&D; intramural vs extramural) | Data as of: {get_data_date(perf_path)}"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("In-house vs granted-out data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

### Where the federal R&D dollar goes

The same money, by department and agency, for the latest fiscal year. The four research-funding agencies are highlighted; the rest is research performed by science-based departments and in-house bodies. The single largest line is the **National Research Council** — the federal government's own network of laboratories — ahead of any individual granting agency, with the Canadian Space Agency, National Defence and the science-based departments (Agriculture, Natural Resources, Environment) filling out the picture.

```{python}
#| echo: false
ba_path = "data/science/statcan_federal_rd_by_agency.csv"
if os.path.exists(ba_path):
    ba = pd.read_csv(ba_path)
    ba = ba[ba["year"] == ba["year"].max()].copy()
    fy = ba["fiscal_year"].iloc[0]
    ba["lbl"] = [f"${v:,.0f}M" for v in ba["nominal"]]
    fig = category_bar(
        ba, "agency", "nominal", xaxis_title="R&D funding ($ millions)",
        tickprefix="$", color=CATEGORICAL_OTHER, height=560,
        highlight={"NSERC", "CIHR", "SSHRC", "CFI"}, highlight_color=CATEGORICAL_COLORS[0],
        text_col="lbl", text_fmt="{}",
        hovertemplate="%{y}: $%{x:,.0f}M<extra></extra>",
        source_note=(f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 27-10-0026-01 "
                     f"(R&D, total expenditures) | Fiscal year {fy}. "
                     f"Highlighted = the research-funding agencies."))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("By-agency data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Download data

- [R&D Expenditure, GERD (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_rd_expenditure.csv) — OECD MSTI
- [Researchers per 1,000 Employed (CSV)](../data/science/oecd_researchers.csv) — OECD MSTI
- [Federal research-funding agencies, NSERC/SSHRC/CIHR/CFI (CSV)](../data/science/statcan_science_funding.csv) — Statistics Canada 27-10-0026-01 (+ 27-10-0005-01 deflator)
- [Federal science & technology R&D by department/agency (CSV)](../data/science/statcan_federal_rd_by_agency.csv) — Statistics Canada 27-10-0026-01
- [Federal R&D, in-house vs granted out (CSV)](../data/science/statcan_federal_rd_by_performer.csv) — Statistics Canada 27-10-0026-01
